skill.name: postmark-automation
skill.description: Automate Postmark transactional email work through Rube MCP. It covers batch template sends, template management, delivery metrics, bounce review, complaints, and server settings.
skill.summary: Run common Postmark email delivery and monitoring workflows through Rube MCP.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static external-command findings are false positives caused by Markdown backticks around tool names, parameters, and examples. The skill intentionally uses a third-party MCP endpoint and Postmark tools that can send email and modify server configuration, so publication should include a clear network and outbound email warning.

medium_findings.title: Networked Email Sending and Server Changes
medium_findings.description: The skill guides an agent to send templated batch emails through Postmark and to edit server settings. This is legitimate for the skill purpose, but misuse could send unwanted email or change delivery behavior.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The documented workflow explicitly includes POSTMARK_SEND_BATCH_WITH_TEMPLATES and POSTMARK_EDIT_SERVER. The risk is contextual rather than malicious because these actions match the declared Postmark automation purpose.

low_findings.title: Third-Party MCP Endpoint Dependency
low_findings.description: The setup directs users to add the Rube MCP endpoint. This is expected for the integration, but it creates a dependency on a third-party service handling tool calls.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The endpoint is clearly documented and aligns with the skill purpose. No evidence found that the endpoint is used for hidden exfiltration.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backtick Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged many Markdown backticks as Ruby or shell execution. The reviewed lines are documentation for MCP tool names, parameters, and example workflow steps, not executable code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: SKILL.md contains prose and fenced Markdown examples only. No evidence found of a script, shell command, Ruby execution, or user-controlled command construction.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Finding Is Not Supported
low_findings.description: The scanner flagged a weak cryptographic algorithm at the description line, but the line only describes Postmark automation. No cryptographic code or hashing logic is present in the reviewed skill file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 is frontmatter text and contains no cryptographic operation. No evidence found for weak algorithm usage elsewhere in the skill file.

dangerous_patterns.title: Outbound Email Capability
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill documents use of Postmark batch template sending. This pattern can contact external recipients and should require user confirmation before sending.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The sending tool is explicitly named in the workflow. The pattern is expected for the skill but still materially affects external recipients.

dangerous_patterns.title: Postmark Server Configuration Changes
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill includes editing server settings such as SMTP API activation, webhooks, open tracking, and link tracking. These changes can affect all future messages on a server.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The server edit workflow and sensitive settings are directly documented. The risk is operational impact, not hidden malicious intent.

content.value_statement: Postmark operations often require many repeated tool calls and careful parameter checks. This skill provides guided workflows for sending, templates, bounces, metrics, and server settings.
actual_capabilities: Guides setup of a Rube MCP Postmark connection.
Lists, validates, edits, and reviews Postmark templates.
Sends batches of templated transactional emails.
Reviews delivery statistics, tracked email counts, bounces, and spam complaints.
Retrieves and edits Postmark server configuration.
Documents parameter names and common pitfalls for Postmark tools.
limitations: Requires an active Rube MCP connection and authenticated Postmark access.
Does not include executable scripts or direct Postmark API code.
Can only use tool schemas available from the connected MCP server.
Email sending and server edits should be confirmed before execution.

use_cases.title: Send Customer Notifications
use_cases.description: Prepare and send templated transactional messages to many recipients after validating model data.

use_cases.title: Maintain Email Templates
use_cases.description: Inspect, update, and validate Postmark templates before production email changes go live.

use_cases.title: Monitor Delivery Health
use_cases.description: Review bounce counts, complaints, opens, clicks, and delivery summaries for a Postmark server.

prompt_templates.title: Check My Postmark Connection
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Postmark automation skill to confirm Rube MCP is connected and my Postmark connection is active.

prompt_templates.title: Validate a Template Before Sending
prompt_templates.prompt: Find the Postmark template named [template name], inspect its variables, and validate it with this sample model data: [sample data].

prompt_templates.title: Review Recent Delivery Issues
prompt_templates.prompt: Review Postmark delivery stats, bounces, and spam complaints from [start date] to [end date]. Summarize the main risks and next actions.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Batch Template Send
prompt_templates.prompt: Prepare a batch templated send for these recipients: [recipient list]. Validate the template, check sender requirements, and ask for confirmation before sending.

output_examples.input: Review bounces from the last seven days.
output_examples.output: A concise delivery report with bounce types, affected recipients, complaint counts, and cleanup recommendations.

output_examples.input: Validate the welcome email template with sample customer data.
output_examples.output: A validation summary showing required variables, missing values, rendering concerns, and whether the template is ready to send.

output_examples.input: Check server tracking settings before a launch.
output_examples.output: A server settings summary covering open tracking, link tracking, webhook URLs, SMTP API status, and recommended confirmations.

best_practices: Search Rube tools first so current Postmark schemas are used.
Validate templates with realistic model data before sending.
Require explicit user confirmation before sending email or editing server settings.
anti_patterns: Do not send batch emails without checking sender verification.
Do not edit templates without preserving fields that must remain unchanged.
Do not change tracking, webhook, or SMTP settings without impact review.

faq.question: Does this skill send email directly?
faq.answer: It guides Postmark sending through Rube MCP tools. A connected Postmark account is required.

faq.question: Does it require Postmark API keys in prompts?
faq.answer: No. The skill expects authentication through the Rube MCP connection flow.

faq.question: Can it manage email templates?
faq.answer: Yes. It documents listing, inspecting, editing, and validating Postmark templates.

faq.question: Can it help with bounce monitoring?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers bounce lists, spam complaints, and delivery statistics.

faq.question: What is the main security concern?
faq.answer: The main concern is authorized but sensitive network activity, including email sending and server changes.

faq.question: Should users confirm actions before execution?
faq.answer: Yes. Users should confirm recipients, templates, and server changes before any sending or configuration update.
